message.content:
Here are some solid, reasonably priced power‑tool brands that are more than good enough for typical household repairs without getting into high‑end contractor pricing:

• Ryobi – One of the best values. Huge 18V tool ecosystem, widely available, good performance for homeowners.  
• Craftsman – Reliable, affordable, and backed by decent warranties. Good for light to medium-duty work.  
• Black+Decker – Very budget‑friendly. Best for occasional use and simple tasks.  
• Kobalt – Lowe’s in‑house brand. Good quality for the price, better than entry‑level but cheaper than pro brands.  
• Harbor Freight’s Bauer or Hercules lines – Surprisingly capable for the cost; good for DIYers who don’t need pro‑grade durability.  
• Skil – Great quality for home use; newer brushless models are strong performers at low prices.  
• Ridgid – Slightly higher tier but still affordable, and their lifetime service agreement (with registration) is a big perk.

If you want the best mix of price and long‑term flexibility, Ryobi or Skil are usually the top picks for most household users. Let me know what jobs you expect to do, and I can recommend specific tools.
